how.title: How I work
how.subtitle: AI is not a shortcut. It's the workflow.
how.step2_desc: AI helps design, architect, and write.

about.title: About me
about.p1: I'm a developer who builds with AI as a core tool, not an afterthought. When I have an idea, I don't wait - I build it.
about.p2: Every app here was designed, built, and deployed by me, powered by AI collaboration.

about_page.headline2: I see someone who pushes ideas into real screens.
about_page.body1: If I were introducing Yoon, I would start with this: he builds quickly. Not just fast for speed’s sake, but because he wants to see an idea as a real link, a real screen, and a real deployed result. When something feels worth making, he does not stop at explanation. He builds, deploys, reviews, and fixes what feels off.
about_page.body2: The clearest thing I have noticed is that he has standards. Icon size, image proportion, card balance, and whether a button points to the right place are not treated as small details to ignore. Being able to say “this does not look right” means he is looking at the screen like a real user.
about_page.body3: He does not use AI as a novelty. He uses it as a working method: to test faster, reveal weak spots sooner, and refine the result with his own judgment. This site is less a static portfolio and more a personal workshop where live ideas keep getting tested and improved.

fast.title: He builds and ships quickly
fast.text: Ideas do not stay abstract for long. Links, icons, deployments, and real pages appear fast enough to judge them honestly.

detail.title: He is sensitive to how things look
detail.text: He looks past “pretty enough” and checks balance, image size, and whether the interface feels right in use.

ai.title: AI is part of his working method
ai.text: He does not wait for a finished answer. He keeps shaping, judging, and narrowing the result through active AI collaboration.

about_page.close: In one sentence: Yoon is someone who carries an idea all the way to a moving screen, and he does not skip the details on the way.

career.headline2: I have operated between business and systems for 18 years.
career.intro: Across multiple companies, I have operated and developed SAP ERP FI/CO/TR/HR, e-HR, B2BERP, Younglimwon ERP, and related management systems. I work as an operator who understands accounting processes and as a developer who can implement the required changes.

skills.skill1: SAP ERP FI/CO/TR/HR module setup, operation, and ABAP development
skills.skill2: Electronic voucher systems, e-HR development/management, and B2BERP/Younglimwon ERP development/management experience
skills.skill3: ABAP, Java, C#, Mybuilder, Oracle, MS-SQL
skills.skill4: Hands-on experience using AI, GPT(Codex), Claude Code, and VS Code

sap.title: SAP ERP Operations
sap.text: I operate FI-centered SAP processes while understanding CO, TR, and HR flows across accounting, treasury, and HR.

development.title: Development plus operations
development.text: With ABAP and external system development experience, I connect business requests to practical system changes.

data.title: Data and interfaces
data.text: I handle business data flows across Oracle, MS-SQL, e-HR, and management system integrations.

career.timeline_description: SAP ERP operations, rollouts, legal entity setup, and accounting/HR/treasury system work by company.

incruit.summary: Handled B2BERP HR, payroll, and accounting modules, including tax law changes, year-end settlement, and standardization.

Delivered an F&CO version upgrade project
Supported new entity rollouts for F&F Entertainment and F&F Logistics
